critical.details: Section 3.1 says encoder permutation; Section 4.5 says decoder permutation. Verify actual implementation. If decoder permutation was used, the 'degeneracy' interpretation is wrong---identical scores are expected. Reframe the finding accordingly.

critical.description: Acknowledge metric collapse due to perfect probe accuracy
critical.details: All hierarchies show resid_acc = sae_acc = 1.0, so absorption = 1 - k_sparse_acc. The metric measures only k-sparse probing difficulty, not SAE encoding loss. This is a fundamental design flaw in the semantic-hierarchy adaptation that must be acknowledged.

major.description: Reframe construct-validity result as 'insufficiently powered' not 'inconclusive'
major.details: With n=7 SAEs and CI spanning 1.37 correlation units, the test is uninformative, not merely inconclusive. 'Inconclusive' implies adequate power; 'insufficiently powered' admits the test design was inadequate.

major.description: Report missing first-letter vs. non-hierarchy correlation
major.details: The statistical_analysis_summary.json contains r=0.218 (CI: [-0.915, 0.852]) for first-letter vs. non-hierarchy. This unreported correlation should be included to avoid selective reporting.
Apply Bonferroni or Benjamini-Hochberg correction across all reported tests (~9 tests). The hierarchy specificity p-value (0.0032) survives Bonferroni (0.0056), which strengthens the claim.

The abstract states the Random-SAE result 'indicates that the semantic-hierarchy adaptation captures artifacts unrelated to learned SAE structure.' This overclaims. Soften to acknowledge the metric's dependence on encoder geometry.

major.description: Acknowledge hierarchy specificity test is confounded by task structure
major.details: The hierarchy condition is multi-class (parent vs. 2-3 children) while non-hierarchy is binary (word A vs. word B). This structural mismatch may explain the observed difference. Acknowledge this limitation.
GPT-2 shows near-zero scores (Standard: 0.000, TopK: 0.003). Check raw k_sparse_acc values. If all near 1.0, acknowledge ceiling effect rather than model-specific behavior.
The observation that acc_sae = acc_resid = 1.0 for all hierarchies (Section 3.4) is buried mid-paragraph. Pull it out as a standalone observation or methodological caveat.

minor.details: Section 5.2: 'non-hierarchy scores are 41% higher than hierarchy scores' should clarify 'relative to the hierarchy mean.'

minor.description: Add bridge sentence between Limitations and Conclusion
minor.details: The transition from Section 5.5 to Section 6 is abrupt. Add a one-sentence bridge.

minor.description: Cite Korznikov et al. (2026) in main paper
minor.details: Connect the Random-SAE finding to the broader literature on SAE metric validity. Currently only cited in alternatives.md.
The paper reports SAEBench evaluator seed=42 but not probe training seeds.

minor.description: Provide exact sentence templates in appendix
minor.details: The synthetic data generation templates are mentioned but not provided, blocking exact reproduction.
Section 2 is ~800 words (20% of body). Condense subsections 2.4 and 2.5 into a single 'Broader Context' subsection.
